Revision Date: 31 Mar 2018
CHAPTER 1.12
LEGISLATIVE SERVICE
(SPECIAL PROVISION) PENSION ORDINANCE
(Ordinances 9 of 2005, 34 of 2016 and Legal Notice 16/2006)
AN ORDINANCE TO PROVIDE FOR A PENSION FOR PERSONS WHO SERVED AS
LEGISLATORS; AND FOR CONNECTED PURPOSES.
Commencement
[1 April 2005]
Short title
1. This Ordinance may be cited as the Legislative Service (Special
Provision) Pension Ordinance.
Pension
2. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in any other Ordinance relating
to the grant of a pension and gratuities, a pension is granted to a person listed in
the Schedule in the amount of $36,000 per annum by equal monthly
instalments, in arrears.
(Amended by Ord. 34 of 2016)
Surviving spouse’s allowance
3. (1) Upon the death of a person referred to in the Schedule, a surviving
spouse shall be paid during his lifetime an annual allowance equivalent to one
half of the pension payable to that person immediately before the date of death.
(2) The surviving spouse of a deceased person referred to in the Schedule
is entitled to receive during his lifetime an annual allowance equivalent to one
half of the pension that would have been payable to that person had the
pensionable spouse survived until the commencement date.
(3) A surviving spouse’s annual allowance under this section shall be
payable in equal monthly instalments in arrears.
Charge on the Consolidated Fund etc.
4. (1) There is charged on and paid out of the Consolidated Fund all such
sums of money as may be granted by way of pension and allowance in
accordance with this Ordinance.
(2) For the avoidance of doubt any entitlement established by this
Ordinance shall take effect on and from the date of the coming into force of this
Ordinance.

Legislative Service
6 CAP. 1.12 (Special Provision) Pension LAWS OF TURKS &
CAICOS ISLANDS
Revision Date: 31 Mar 2018
Power to amend Schedule
5. (1) The Governor may, by Order published in the Gazette, amend the
Schedule.
(2) No amendment shall take effect under subsection (1) unless the Order
thereof has been laid before the House of Assembly and approved by a
resolution of the House.
